---
title: "Statistical Data Visualization"
site: distill::distill_website
author:
- name: Kris Sankaran
  affiliation: University of Wisconsin-Madison
description: |
  STAT 479 Spring 2020
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

This is a course about the use of visualization in the data science workflow.
You will gain hands-on experience with developing visualization to support,

* Data cleaning and tidying
* Exploratory analysis
* Model inspection
* Scientific reporting

We will mostly use R, though occasionally we will use the vega-lite library in
javascript. Prior experience with R, but not javascript, is expected.

In terms of content, we'll begin with a three week crash course on the
fundamental principles of data visualization. This will provide a vocabulary
that we can use to design and critique visualizations throughout the semester.
Weeks 4 - 5 are focused on the connections between visualization and tidy data.
It's a two way street. Rearranging your data in the right way can make it much
easier to visualize; conversely, a good interface can immediately highlight data
quality issues.

From here on, we'll focus on exploratory analysis and model visualization. Weeks
6 - 8 consider data with temporal, geographic, or network structure -- each
comes with its own visual vocabulary. Weeks 9 - 11 focus on visualization of
high-dimensional data, especially those that leverage methods from unsupervised
learning. Weeks 12 and 13 describe the recent use of visualization to support
inspection of complex supervised models. We close on notes about the role of
visualization within the wider (data) scientific enterprise.

## Syllabus

This will be an asynchronous class, with short video recordings released in
advance. There will be an optional discussion every week. Online discussion on
the course piazza is encouraged.

Structurally, the main types of work in this class are

* Homeworks. These will be released every other Friday, and focus on practical
  skill development.
* Exams. There will be two midterms (open book, timed).
* Group Project. This is an opportunity to explore a dataset or visualization
  algorithm.

The official syllabus, which gives requirements, learning objectives, and
grading mechanisms, is available
[here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UWK2-tZ5vn9Nc0fez791npFQqomZNs-7/view?usp=sharing).

## Live Sessions

Office Hours:
(Optional) Discussion:

## Homework

Submissions must be made on [Canvas](tbd).

* Homework 1 (Due: )

## Readings

| Week | Topic | Reading | Notes | Lectures |
| - | - | --- | - | - |
| 1 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/encoding.png"/> | Marks and Channels | [Introduction to Data Science: Chapter 7](https://rafalab.github.io/dsbook) <br/> [Data Types, Graphical Marks, and Visual Encoding Channels](https://observablehq.com/@uwdata/data-types-graphical-marks-and-visual-encoding-channels?collection=@uwdata/visualization-curriculum) | | |
| 1 (optional) | | [Data Humanism](http://giorgialupi.com/data-humanism-my-manifesto-for-a-new-data-wold)<br/> [Automation Makes Us Dumb](https://www.wsj.com/articles/automation-makes-us-dumb-1416589342)| | |
| 2 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/faceting.png"/>| Faceting and Layout | [Introduction to Data Science: Chapter 8](https://rafalab.github.io/dsbook)<br/> [Multi-View Composition](https://observablehq.com/@uwdata/multi-view-composition?collection=@uwdata/visualization-curriculum) | | |
| 3 <br/> <img width=250 src="interaction.png"/> | Interaction | [Visualization Analysis and Design: Chapter 11](https://search.library.wisc.edu/catalog/9911196629502121)<br/> [Interaction](https://observablehq.com/@uwdata/interaction?collection=@uwdata/visualization-curriculum) | | |
| 3 | (optional) | [Up and Down the Ladder of Abstraction](http://worrydream.com/LadderOfAbstraction/)  | | |
| 4 <img width=250 src="assets/tidy.png"/> | Data Wrangling | [R for Data Science: Chapter 12](https://r4ds.had.co.nz)| | |
| 5 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/diagnostics.png"/> | Quality Checking | [Getting Started with naniar](https://naniar.njtierney.com/articles/getting-started-w-naniar.html) <br/> [Profiler: Integrated Statistical Analysis and Visualization for Data Quality Assessment](https://idl.cs.washington.edu/files/2012-Profiler-AVI.pdf) | | |
| 6 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/ts.png"/> | Time Series | [Forecasting Principles and Practice: Chapter 2](https://otexts.com/fpp3/graphics.html) | | |
| 7 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/geo.png"/> | Geographic Data | [Geographic Data in R: Chapter 2](https://geocompr.robinlovelace.net/spatial-class.html) | | |
| 8 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/network.png"/>| Network Data | [Visualization Analysis and Design: Chapter 9](https://search.library.wisc.edu/catalog/9911196629502121) [Modern Statistics for Modern Biology: Chapter 10.1 - 10.3](http://web.stanford.edu/class/bios221/book/Chap-Graphs.html)::| | |
| 9 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/clusters.png"/> | Clustering | [Intro to Data Science: Chapter 34](https://rafalab.github.io/dsbook)<br/> [Superheat Vignette (2, 3 & 6)](https://rlbarter.github.io/superheat/basic-usage.html) <br/> [Cluster Analysis of Genomic Data](http://users.umiacs.umd.edu/~hcorrada/CMSC702/readings/Solutions_ch13.pdf) | | |
| 10 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/dimred1.png"/>| Dimensionality Reduction I | [Beginner's Guide to Dimensionality Reduction](https://idyll.pub/post/visxai-dimensionality-reduction-1dbad0a67a092b007c526a45/)  <br/> [An Illustrated Introduction to the t-SNE algorithm](https://github.com/oreillymedia/t-SNE-tutorial) <br/> [How to use t-SNE effectively](https://distill.pub/2016/misread-tsne/) | | |
| 11 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/dimred2.png"/>| Dimensionality Reduction II | [Text mining with R: Chapter 6](https://www.tidytextmining.com/topicmodeling.html) <br/> [Termite: Visualization Techniques for Assessing Textual Topic Models](https://d1wqtxts1xzle7.cloudfront.net/30738409/2012-Termite-AVI.pdf?1362144247=&response-content-disposition=inline%3B+filename%3DTermite_Visualization_Techniques_for_Ass.pdf&Expires=1608157711&Signature=OiLaMpnhwGe~4yVIW4bQScUEjvFm-mH7wBqWjNKA47g4jdzWtyMdEE2X-WkuEUwVLb7IGQzy0MfV~oI8Y47XT33OopRWJLFPGjSq15dCjSCAoORTqQMcBkKMQ-rB84V-qJN40cP4nbF8DP7pYeUun8zAxnQzHY1tzd4t3tL~hY1HlVy05jedJfgMUdZwweKOW9G6SwALqsGEXPiixPV-FTdVOrwwcxedccDAAsGK~PXY0J4w3GlumFQJwY8o45bWjycIiUJ8WWKEjIfkat~Hefh85eDGlnXUl3BmjlUSAiayRBtqTtJL2YMM2puf-GZafTMW7XnTnzhBysz1GWBKvw__&Key-Pair-Id=APKAJLOHF5GGSLRBV4ZA) <br/> [Visualizing the structure of RNA-seq expression data using grade of membership models](https://journals.plos.org/plosgenetics/article?id=10.1371/journal.pgen.1006599) | | |
| 12 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/supervised.png"/>| Visualizing Supervised Models | [Towards better analysis of machine learning models: A visual analytics perspective](https://arxiv.org/abs/1702.01226) <br/> [Interacting with Predictions: Visual Inspection of Black-box Machine Learning Models](https://josuakrause.github.io/info/prospector.html) | | |
| 13 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/dl.png"/>| Visualizing Deep Learning Models | [Four Experiments in Handwriting with a Neural Network](https://distill.pub/2016/handwriting/) <br/> [Towards Better Analysis of Deep Convolutional Networks](https://arxiv.org/abs/1604.07043) | | |
| 14 <br/> <img width=250 src="assets/community.png"/>| Conclusion | [655 Frustrations of Doing Data Visualization](https://www.datasketch.es/project/655-frustrations-doing-data-visualization) <br/> [Tukey, Design Thinking, and Better Questions](https://simplystatistics.org/2019/04/17/tukey-design-thinking-and-better-questions/) <br/> [As We May Think](https://www.theatlantic.com/magazine/archive/1945/07/as-we-may-think/303881/) | | |
